text: Stopped in for some Gelato for my nephew since it was so hot out. I understand this stuff melts, but why did it seem like it was melting before she scooped it? the gelato did not look appetizing one bit.
key_point: The gelato seemed to be melting before it was scooped, making it unappetizing.
business_response: We apologize for the disappointing appearance of our gelato. Our gelato is made fresh in-house daily, and we take extra precautions to ensure it's stored at the right temperature. We'd love to have you and your nephew try it again and experience the quality we strive for.

text: We ordered food to go last night and though we regularly eat at other Native locations this was the first time we were disappointed.  The garbage pizza was barely warm and didn't have very many toppings for being a loaded pizza.  The wedge salad had hardly any dressing on it, or bacon and blue cheese crumbles,and was the part of our order we had to wait for after arriving which may be why the pizza was cold.  The biggest disappointment though were the wings.  We ordered half honey BBQ and half mild traditional.  We weren't told that mild meant a butter sauce.  That is not traditional by any means.  When we got home and discovered how our order looked we called and the manager offered to make it right and said we should have been told what \"mild\" meant.  We are able to go in and have the
key_point: The wings were mislabeled as 'mild traditional' but actually came with a butter sauce, not traditional seasoning.
business_response: We apologize for the confusion with our wing flavors. We should have clearly communicated that our 'mild' wings come with a butter sauce. We're glad we could make it right for you and look forward to serving you a more accurate order on your next visit.

text: 2/2/11- Talk about going downhill! Dunno know if it is a new mgr or what but when I get a roll with my salad and the bottom half is covered in black mold...EEK! \n\nThis little stuck-in-the-seventies place is the restaurant at the Belair Golf Course off of Bell Rd. Its really somewhat of a hidden gem though if you're looking for regular ol' American food. Its quiet, has a lovely view of the lake, peaceful, good service and above average food. The breakfast is especially good with the chicken fried steak being my personal favorite. Its actually real steak not some pre-breaded b.s. I like the club sandwich for lunch- generous amounts of meat, fresh lettuce & tomato, hot fries- how can you go wrong? My only reason for not giving it another star would be the decor. Its perfectly fine, I guess.
key_point: the bottom half of my roll was covered in black mold
business_response: We take food safety very seriously and are deeply sorry to hear that a moldy roll was served to you. We will retrain our staff on proper food handling procedures to prevent this from happening again. We invite you to return and experience our improved standards.

text:
We were greeted at the door by a young lady letting me know it would be a 20 min wait for a booth.  The place is a bit small and looks like it may fit about 100?

Once inside Angel greeted us with our menus. 

Food came to us QUICK!! 

We had:

Peanut butter waffle - it's peanut butter chips melted on waffles. yum!!
Plain waffle - butter n maple syrup soooo good!
Texas eggs and bacon sandwich was delicious
Scrambled eggs - plain variety
Eggs over medium - PERFECTLY MADE!! 
Grits - kinda clumpy
Hashbrowns - processed, but none the less fine
Coffee - great cup of cafe Java

Angel is an awesome server, though the cashier mentioned she from another House... Just helping out here as most of their crew is in Vegas for a wedding.  They need to snatch Angel up! She was super busy
key_point: Grits were clumpy
business_response: We're glad you enjoyed your meal overall, and we're sorry to hear that our grits didn't meet your expectations. We'll work on perfecting our grits recipe to ensure they're smooth and enjoyable for all our guests. We appreciate your feedback and hope to see you again soon.

text: The rooms are immaculate and unbelievably comfortable.  As the newest hotel on the strip, it lives up to its 5-Star backing.  Though, minus a star for the blood sucking black jack dealers downstairs.  They'll provide you with a steady dose of 14's, 15's and 16's.  I had one dealer call me her \"Bust it Baby\" and it's not because I look like Plies.
key_point: The blackjack dealers downstairs were aggressive and made derogatory comments, including calling the reviewer 'Bust it Baby'.
business_response: We apologize for the unacceptable behavior of our blackjack dealers, particularly the comment made by one of our staff members. We take all complaints of this nature seriously and will address this issue with our team to ensure a more respectful and enjoyable experience for all our guests.

text: I normally enjoy Pita Jungle, but this was our first visit to this location. I don't think we'll return. The Spanakopita was barely warm, and I had to double check that they actually used fillo dough. It certainly didn't look like it at first glance, and it did not have much of a flaky crunch. It absolutely was not worth nearly $4 for a very modest halved triangle. My sirloin with hummus was simply not appetizing- the two flavors did not  marry well to my taste, and the meat was too overdone (and I was not given an option to have it rarer). The chicken with hummus and pine nuts is far superior and a dish I have enjoyed many times before. Hubby enjoyed his falafel pita well enough. His only complaint was that there was not enough tzatsiki sauce. I thought the falafel was overly crunchy and
key_point: The Spanakopita was barely warm, and it did not have much of a flaky crunch.
business_response: We're sorry to hear that our Spanakopita didn't meet your expectations. We'll review our preparation process to ensure our pastries are served at the right temperature and with the flaky crunch you expect. We'd love to have you back to try it again.

text: Charming little cafe with a unique and delicious menu item- crepes. I love the sweet crepes and I always order the stawberry with nutella crepe. The coffees are delicious too. I have been here about 6 times recently and while the staff are very friendly, it seems they are always short 1-2 people during the mornings and weekends. Typical wait time for me when I dine alone is about 30-40 minutes. Just be prepared for that, enjoy a coffee and the atmosphere.
key_point: staff are always short 1-2 people during mornings and weekends, causing a 30-40 minute wait time
business_response: We appreciate your loyalty and feedback. We're working to ensure our staff levels meet demand, especially during peak hours. We invite you to return and enjoy a shorter wait time, on us, next time you visit.

text: Tried this place out and the food was ok, what concerned me was the cleanliness of the kitchen. The floor was really dirty, under the burners or stove seemed to have months of grease buildup etc. 50c extra to use debit or credit card btw.
key_point: The kitchen floor had months of grease buildup under the burners or stove.
business_response: We take the cleanliness of our kitchen very seriously and will address the issue with our maintenance team to ensure our floors are properly cleaned and maintained. We appreciate your feedback and invite you to give us another chance to serve you better.

text: If I could, I would give this place zero stars! The food was not bad but if you like to be treated rudely and enjoy crappy service then this is a place for you! Right when we were seated my husband wanted to get up and leave but we were in a large group so out of respect for everyone else who was hungry in our group we stayed.
key_point: The server treated us rudely from the start, making us want to leave immediately after being seated.
business_response: We're sorry to hear that our server's behavior fell short of our usual standards. We'll address this with our team to ensure a more welcoming experience for all guests. We'd love the opportunity to serve you better in the future.

text:
Yum! Who doesn't like a good panini? Thanks again to Groupon, got to try out a place I wouldn't have normally sought out. Thank you too to the Yelpers for the advice on its location. Definately tucked in next to Handlebar Jay's.

Went on a late Sunday evening, super slow. Very nice and casual bartender who also ended up being our cook and server. Out of a few things we wanted to try (the gorgonzolla/fig app) which was a bummer but his recommendations made up for it. Ended up having the goat cheese & slow roasted tomatoes crustini. YUM!!! I had the chicken panini, date had itallian press. Both were enjoyed and had leftovers for the next day. 

Made a special trip across town for this place. I would go back but just wish it were closer.
key_point: super slow service on a late Sunday evening, despite a nice and casual bartender who also served as cook and server
business_response: We appreciate your feedback and apologize for the slow service on your late Sunday evening visit. We're glad our bartender's recommendations made up for the wait, and we're happy you enjoyed the goat cheese & slow roasted tomatoes crustini and your chicken panini. We'll work to improve our efficiency to ensure a smoother experience for all our guests.

text: I heard they were very good at catering to food allergens, but we had a pretty poor experience.  Their gluten free menu was really limited compared to their normal menu.  I have a soy intolerance, and they brought me crab in a soy wrapper.  We didn't figure this out until I was a couple of bites in.  Then they didn't discount my meal.  The waiter took forever even though the place was pretty empty.  Lame all around.
key_point: They brought me crab in a soy wrapper despite my soy intolerance, which wasn't discovered until I had eaten a couple of bites.
business_response: We fell short of our promise to accommodate your soy intolerance, and for that, we're truly sorry. We'll review our procedures to ensure our staff is more vigilant about allergen warnings. We'd love the chance to serve you better in the future.

text: I really like the atmosphere of this pub when we first went there.  I liked how small and intimate it was.  I like dark, candle lit bars.  It just makes me feel a little more cozy. Unfortunately, the service was a bit lacking.  It took about 10 minutes to get a beer (on several occasions) when there was hardly anyone there, and they charged us twice for 2 single Jameson's. We didn't catch this until we had gotten home. It's not that big of a deal, but considering we went on a night when it wasn't that busy, you think they would be paying more attention to what was going on in front of the bar (i.e. people waiting for drinks) and behind the bar (i.e. getting someone's tab right) I have a feeling we'll be going back. It was a great time regardless, but I guess there are some things that just
key_point: It took about 10 minutes to get a beer when there was hardly anyone there, and they charged us twice for 2 single Jameson's.
business_response: We apologize for the slow service and billing error during your recent visit. We're taking steps to improve our attention to detail, especially during less busy times, and to ensure accurate orders are fulfilled promptly. We'd love to have you back and show you the level of service you deserve.

text:
Occupying what used to be Nicolson's (where some of Harry Potter was apparently penned) this is a welcome change back from the site's most recent use as a Chinese buffet restaurant. The space is well lit, particularly by the setting sun at this time of year, and furnished in an understated yet quirky fashion.


 The service is prompt and pleasant and the range of wines well priced, ranging from £10.95 to around the £25 mark. Unusually for a modest establishment of this sort they stock a dessert wine but that's to be commended.


 Opting for the pre-theatre menu (£9.95 for two courses, £12.95 for three) my starter of potted duck was an ample size although more than one pickle would have been an improvement. My companion's soup of the day was pronounced a success al
key_point: The potted duck starter was an ample size, but could have benefited from more than one pickle.
business_response: We're glad you enjoyed your potted duck starter, and we'll take your feedback on the pickles to heart. We'll make sure to add a few extra pickles to our recipe for future guests. Thank you for dining with us, and we look forward to welcoming you back soon.

text:
This is such a classic restaurant. I love going here after a little Forum shopping. I have never had anything bad and the service is always excellent.

My favorite appetizer is the Sesame Seared Ahi Tuna and the Carpaccio of Beef Tenderloin- Delicious! I always get the Beefsteak Tomato Capri because it is my all time favorite salad. The Linguine and Clams Sauce is excellent if you are looking for a pasta dish. The Chilean Sea Bass is also a great choice for a light fish dinner.

For dessert the Key Lime Pie is a must. This restaurant will not disappoint.
key_point: The reviewer loves the Sesame Seared Ahi Tuna and the Carpaccio of Beef Tenderloin as their favorite appetizers.
business_response: We're thrilled to hear that our Sesame Seared Ahi Tuna and Carpaccio of Beef Tenderloin have become your go-to appetizers. We're proud to serve dishes that exceed our customers' expectations, and we can't wait to welcome you back for another delicious meal.

text: I LOVE sushi! And I love it even more when it is from Tokyo Lobby.  I eat here at least once a week, budget be damned.  I can't help it. I'm addicted.  I probably need some kind of support group. I'm all about the soft shell crab and the blue fin. I am also a big fan of their bento boxes.  You get a lot for your buck.  And for appetizers, the Heart Attack is great (never in a million years would I have thought I would be paying for a heart attack).  I usually go as they are opening, because once lunch hour hits, an available seat will be impossible. You should really give this place a try, it is a real treat.
key_point: The soft shell crab and blue fin sushi are standout favorites, and the bento boxes offer great value for the price.
business_response: Thank you for your loyalty and enthusiasm for our sushi offerings, particularly our soft shell crab and blue fin options. We're glad you appreciate the value in our bento boxes. We're happy to have you as a regular customer and look forward to serving you again soon.

text: I used to live in Thailand, and this is one of my top choices when I'm on this side of the ocean. Friendly people and the most authentic Thai food I've found in Phoenix.
key_point: The most authentic Thai food I've found in Phoenix
business_response: We're thrilled to hear that our authentic Thai dishes remind you of home. Our team takes pride in serving traditional flavors that transport our customers to Thailand. We'd love to welcome you back for another delicious meal!

text:
When I moved to S Scottsdale from Central Phoenix, I was so sad to leave Richardson's and Dick's Hideaway behind in my old hood . . .  until I discovered Carlsbad Tavern. 

I love my food with a little bite - it's comfort AND adventure on one plate. And unlike my central favorites, Carlsbad has plenty of tables ready for me to pop in on a whim. More room, yet it still feels cozy.

Outside by the waterfalls with warming heaters - great for a first date OR a night with good friends!
key_point: Carlsbad Tavern has plenty of tables ready for spontaneous visits, unlike the reviewer's previous favorites in Central Phoenix
business_response: We're thrilled to hear that our spacious yet cozy atmosphere makes it easy for you to pop in on a whim. We're glad you can enjoy a great meal with friends or on a first date, and we'll keep working to ensure our tables are always available for spontaneous visits.

text:
When Stacy Phipps opened the doors to his Indian School establishment in 2008, I was his #1 fan. Stacy catered my holiday functions, family gatherings, and weeknight dinners for the next four years. I was heartbroken to see him leave the neighborhood. I sat back and watched sadly as the quality of food suffered under new management, and as the smokehouse finally shut it's doors last year.

When I found out Stacy was back, I was ecstatic! But there are some clear differences, and as much as I want to scream a five star review from the rooftops, I don't think I can just yet. As with most jilted Arcadian food lovers, he's going to have to work to earn my trust back. So today we begin with 3.5 stars, and hopefully we can work our way up.

On the pro side: the interior has improved, the ama
key_point: the pork ribs were microwaved, which was a disappointment because I'm used to hot meat off the smoker
business_response: We apologize for the inconsistency in our smoking process, and we're taking steps to ensure that all meats are cooked to perfection, just like our signature brisket. We appreciate your feedback and would love to have you back to try our smoked meats, cooked to order, and experience the improvements we've made.

text: I don't usually dole out 1 star reviews but this place was really awful.  The signature scallop dish recommended by our server was small and below Red Lobster quality (think Shoney's or something along those lines), the chicken pot pie tasted like a frozen meat pie.  No joke, it was really lousy and the loud mouth celebrity chef should be ashamed to have his name attached to it.  It's a restaurant worthy of a reality show and perhaps that's the point because I can't think of a better reason to open a new restaurant in the middle of a busy casino that is so awful and completely overpriced for the poor quality food served up.  Service was equally uninspired, the girl that brought our dishes to us was flat out frightening and surly, our main server seemed completely out of it as well.  Overal
key_point: The signature scallop dish was small and below Red Lobster quality, tasting like something from Shoney's.
business_response: We're sorry to hear our signature scallop dish didn't meet your expectations. We'll review our preparation and presentation to ensure it meets the standards our customers have come to expect from us. We invite you to return and experience the improvements we're making.

text: Pretty good boutique.  Back in the day, the used to carry guys' clothing - basically the local stuff like Spraygraphic.  They had an epic sale when they decided to get rid of all of their men's clothing and I was able to get some good shirts for cheap.\n\nI like the way it's laid out inside and everyone was nice.
key_point: The store used to carry guys' clothing, including local brands like Spraygraphic, but no longer does.
business_response: We appreciate your fond memories of our past inventory, particularly our local brands like Spraygraphic. We're glad you were able to snag some great deals during our clearance sale. We're always looking for ways to refresh our selection, and we'd love to have you back to see what's new.

text:
From the moment we walked in the door we were treated like celebrities. I wish I was one after leaving here! The food was some of the best Thai we've ever had, and I live in Chicago and have tasted dishes more than a dozen different authentic Thai restaurants. 

We never were without a glass of water or a full glass of wine. I wish I lived here so I could make this a regular stop during my weekly Thai and Sushi cravings. 

The Tilapia was out of this world - topped off with a delicious medley of mint, onions and lemon leaves. I highly recommend the Spring Rolls - very fresh and tasty! Give this place a try - you won't be disappointed.
key_point: The Tilapia was out of this world - topped off with a delicious medley of mint, onions and lemon leaves.
business_response: We're thrilled you enjoyed our signature Tilapia dish, and we're glad our chef's fresh mint, onion, and lemon leaf topping impressed you. We appreciate your recommendation and look forward to welcoming you back for more authentic Thai flavors.

text: This is my favorite brunch spot in town. Bottomless Bubbles with fresh fruit juice, fluffy omelettes and Bang Bang Shrimp. Been here a dozen times and never disappointed.  Their scallops and filet are out of this world. Happy Hour is also not to be missed with fresh Raspberry Collins, Bang Bang Tacos and friendly bartenders.   It's the service that really stands out. I always sit in the bar area and without fail have had staff who aren't just great servers, but really add to the experience. Love, love, love.
key_point: friendly bartenders in the bar area add to the experience
business_response: We're thrilled to hear that our bartenders in the bar area have made a lasting impression on you. We'll continue to recognize and reward our team members who go above and beyond to create a memorable experience. We can't wait to welcome you back for another Happy Hour!

text: Had breakfast this morning. Prices are a little high for what you get.  Very disappointed that food was only Luke warm but it took quite awhile to get our food that we did't send it back.  Waitress was great. \n\nThis was the 2nd time we've been there and both times food was not hot. Not a cozy place as it is open high ceilings when tend to be noisy.
key_point: Food was only Luke warm, despite taking quite a while to arrive.
business_response: We're sorry to hear that your breakfast was not hot when it arrived. We'll review our kitchen procedures to ensure that dishes are served at the right temperature. We'd love to have you back to show you the improvements we're making.

text: Wonderful little cheese shop with a variety of cheeses you've never heard of or heard of but never tasted. You can buy small pieces - orphans, they call them - so that you can taste without spending a bundle. There are gifts for foodies, too: jams, confits, crackers, etc. Really friendly staff and great sandwiches. Wish this had been around during my days at UW-Madison.
key_point: You can buy small pieces, called 'orphans', so that you can taste without spending a bundle.
business_response: We're glad you enjoyed our 'orphan' cheese selection, allowing you to taste a variety of cheeses without breaking the bank. We appreciate your feedback and hope you'll return to explore more of our offerings.

text:
Right off the bat I have to ask what is up with this Stepford Wives hostess staff?  Ok, rhetorical question time over.

I haven't done an itemized pro/con review in while so here we go...

Pros:
Excellent portions of my surf & turf or lobster & filet
Well prepared medium rare steak
Lobster was perfect and I am not a lobster fan
Very good serving staff pair
Well made drinks...martinis and chocolate cakes
Dark, intimate decor (clean-sporty executive clubhouse like)

Cons:
Intimate but noisy.  (I don't like to have to shout over dinner and I want to hear the specials the first time my server fires off with them)*
One cocktail server in the bar/waiting area?  Ridiculous. (I literally batted my eyelashes at a girl I guessed was a cocktail server)
Neither of our servers suggested
key_point: Intimate but noisy atmosphere, with clanking silverware and drawers near the seating area
business_response: We appreciate your feedback on the noise level in our dining area. We're sorry that the proximity to our server station detracted from your experience. We'll look into rearranging our layout to minimize noise and ensure a more comfortable atmosphere for all our guests.

text: Stayed here 11/15-11/17 and I wouldn't mind coming back. Paid a little over $300 for two nights, not too shabby, probably because it wasn't Comedy Festival time. The location is great which was very important to my club hoppin, heel wearin self. Like the other Yelpers mentioned, the bed was very soft, as if I were trapped inside a marshmallow. Decent eating options, still didn't get to try the Pho place but tried Kahunaville. The only thing I have to complain about are the surly workers but I don't blame them.
key_point: The bed was very soft, as if I were trapped inside a marshmallow.
business_response: We're glad you enjoyed your stay and found our location convenient for your club-hopping adventures. We'll take your feedback on our beds seriously and work on finding the perfect balance of comfort and support. We'd love to have you back and try our Pho place next time.

text: Went here for an office event about a month ago.  Service was really great, food was pretty good and ambiance was fine for this type of group event. We initially sat at the communal table on the second floor but ended up migrating to the area under the stairs as it allowed us to network. \n\nThe servers were very flexible and kept the drinks flowing. The food was great - simple pub fare but overall well done. Loved the eggplant option, hummus, calamari, wings, and other dishes to share.
key_point: The servers were very flexible and kept the drinks flowing.
business_response: We're glad to hear our servers provided excellent service, keeping your drinks flowing throughout your event. We'll continue to train our staff to be flexible and attentive to our guests' needs.

text: MEAN people.. Our dog is a customer, called at 11:00AM on a Saturday, with our dog howling so loud that you could hear him a block away.  We knew it was his time to put him down.  However, Matthews refused because they were closing at noon.  Can you believe that?  They said to take him to an emergency clinic and we told them we could not afford that.  We had already checked prices weeks before, because we knew it was getting close. We had already been sedating him and giving him pain meds. So,I called again at 11:30 to beg them to take him.  While I was on the phone they mistook me for another customer and said that it was okay, Dr. Pritchett would wait to see their dog, which they were putting down.  Now, that was biased! It was okay for them, but not for us.  Obviously, Dr. Pritchard was
key_point: Matthews refused to take in our dog at 11:00AM on a Saturday because they were closing at noon
business_response: We're deeply sorry for the pain and distress caused by our closure timing on that difficult day. We should have made an exception for your pet's urgent needs. We'll review our policies to ensure we can provide more compassionate care in similar situations in the future.

text: Seems like a nice place but there are not many vegan options. Even the Veggie Burger comes with mayo and a non vegan bun.\n\nI had the black bean veggie chili and a salad without cheese.  Both were delicious and the presentation was excellent.  I would rate Eat higher, if there were a few more vegan selections.
key_point: The Veggie Burger comes with mayo and a non-vegan bun, limiting vegan options.
business_response: We appreciate your feedback and apologize for the non-vegan bun on our Veggie Burger. We're happy to offer a vegan bun option upon request. We'll work on expanding our vegan menu to better serve our customers.

text: Love the salad bar here ! The best deal is the manager special available all the time where u can get half sandwich and a side salad(well it's a full salad bar actually) all for about 8 dollars ! Salad bar has some high quality organic veggie and greens and it is better than the over priced 13 dollars salad at capital grill I went. Love it here! \nOne star less because the sandwich is only average. Oh don't forget the free ice-cream.
key_point: The sandwich is only average.
business_response: We're glad you enjoyed our salad bar and manager's special, and we appreciate your feedback on our sandwiches. We'll take this as an opportunity to revisit our sandwich offerings to ensure they meet our high standards. We hope to see you again soon and serve you a better sandwich.

text: Went tonight for a takeout order so we could watch Monday Night Football. Order and paid for extra celery & blue cheese( price went up to $1 each! )... Got the order and took it home. The wife wanted to know where the blue cheese was. There was four celery and ONE blue cheese! I was pissed and called them. I drove 3 miles back, and Chelsey who took the order and answered when I called back, told me I was wrong and she personally put three blue cheese in the bag. \n\nDo not ever tell a customer he is a liar. This is what she did. I am about ready to call the manager. My friends do not go here anymore but I've always defended this restaurant. Tonight makes me want to change my mind.
key_point: Chelsey told the customer he was wrong about the blue cheese, which was not in the order as agreed
business_response: We fell short of our promise to include extra blue cheese in your takeout order. We're truly sorry for the miscommunication with Chelsey, who should have apologized and made it right instead of disputing your claim. We'd love to make it up to you with a complimentary meal on your next visit.

text: Yes the servers are beautiful.  The food is decent.  Good happy hour specials.  A large Blue Moon for $6.50.  The sliders were good.  They didn't come as I ordered.  The waitress never noticed she didn't bring a side order.  The food overall was ok to good.  The sweet potato fries were the only bad item.  I know, hard to mess up sweet potato fries, but they did.  Overall if you want a fun atmosphere and don't have high expectations, you will enjoy the Tilted Kilt.
key_point: The sliders were good but didn't come as I ordered
business_response: We're sorry to hear our sliders didn't meet your expectations. We'll review our ordering process to ensure accuracy. We'd love to have you back to try our sliders again, on the house this time.

text: Apparently asking for a tostada salad without cheese is to much to ask for. I asked for one without any dairy for a lactose intolerant person in the drive through. The top had no cheese but once they digged into the salad it had cheese. This is the second time this happened at this location. I will not go back to this location.
key_point: The drive-through staff incorrectly added cheese to a tostada salad ordered without dairy for a lactose intolerant person.
business_response: We apologize for the mistake with your tostada salad, which was supposed to be dairy-free. We'll retrain our drive-through staff to ensure our lactose intolerant customers receive their orders accurately. We'd love to welcome you back and serve you correctly next time.

text: Came here on a Friday morning around 1030 and was greeted the second I parked my car which was wonderful and great service! Sadly the second I came up to the counter a bigger older guy, I'm assuming manager or a higher role, was completely rude! I asked how much an alignment was and explained I had a coupon that gave me a discount 10% and asked how much it would be with that included. His answer was completely rude an belittling \"well I said 89.99 so 10% of that is your price\". I wasn't asking for a math lesson just your pricing.\nThankfully he was given a phone call and Benny continued to help me out with a smile. I realized I left my drink in the car and asked if I could get it and was accommodated nicely. \nOver all this shop was clean, mostly friendly besides the older large gentlema
key_point: The manager, a bigger older guy, was completely rude when explaining the price of an alignment after I mentioned a 10% discount coupon.
business_response: We apologize for the manager's behavior and appreciate your feedback. We'll make sure to provide a more welcoming experience for all customers, including those with coupons. We're glad Benny and the rest of the team provided great service and look forward to your next visit.

text: Good local sub shop.
key_point: Good local sub shop with no specific complaints or compliments mentioned
business_response: We're glad to hear you enjoy our local sub shop! We're always looking for ways to improve, so please let us know if there's anything specific we can do for you in the future.

text: Walked in on a Sat. night about 3 weeks ago and headed right to the bar. Sat there for a good 10-15 min. with both bartenders looking at me but not acknowledging me. Finally, one says \"did you want to order something?\" .... I said \"that was the plan\" and then he says \"oh, why didn't the hostess give you a menu? I hate it when they do that\".... Ok, I've walked into a lot of restaurants and headed right to the bar and Never has a hostess chased me down to give me a menu... It was a lame excuse to make up for the fact that I was being ignored. Ordered a glass of wine and the Meat & Cheese board, nothing special.
key_point: The bartender ignored me for 10-15 minutes before finally acknowledging me, then blamed the hostess for not giving me a menu.
business_response: We're sorry to hear that you felt ignored at the bar. Our hostess should have provided you with a menu as soon as you sat down, and our bartender should have checked in with you sooner. We'll work on improving our communication and service to ensure a better experience for all our guests.

text: They do have a nice menu with tons of 'chocolaty' desserts, which are all amazing on your tongue, oh god! But I tend to prefer to grab a hot chocolate for take-out instead of drinking it on place: too crowdy for me.\n\nOtherwise, a nice place to discover tons of meals with chocolate in all forms! For the chocolate fans only ;)
key_point: the hot chocolate is too crowdy to drink on-site
business_response: We appreciate your feedback and understand that our busy atmosphere may not be ideal for enjoying a hot chocolate on-site. We'd be happy to have you try our take-out option for a more relaxed experience.

text: I was disappointed to see the prior Yelp page removed - why? because it was accurate? This place (when it was at its prior location way back when) used to be cool, when there was a sense of community. They had flyers, local periodicals. Not anymore. And, not only that, they do readings directly in front of the tarot deck cabinet - l literally have to walk around the table to get to the tarot cards - so, I'm minding my own business looking for a deck, and I have to hear someone's personal stuff like. Um...hello? Isn't that a little bit unethical? \"For entertainment value\" is one thing, but would you YouTube your appointments with your financial adviser or physician for everyone to see? I mean, c'mon...show some *bleep* respect for the people coming in to your store and give them a little
key_point: readings are held directly in front of the tarot deck cabinet, making it difficult for customers to browse without hearing personal conversations
business_response: We appreciate your feedback and apologize for any discomfort caused by our reading area layout. We will consider relocating our readings to a more private space to ensure a respectful experience for all customers.

text:
I'm writing this at the bar at BRI. I just finished the stacked enchilada. So very good!! It was pretty spicy but not in an overwhelming way. Super tasty!! 

The other night I had the Spanglish sandwich. That was another huge winner!! 

The beer selection is pretty good too!

You should check this spot out!
key_point: The stacked enchilada was very good and not too spicy, with a super tasty flavor.
business_response: We're thrilled you enjoyed our stacked enchilada, and we're glad you found the spice level just right. We're proud of our menu offerings, and it sounds like we hit the mark with you. We'd love to have you back for another delicious meal!

text: Spazzing bartender started a shouting match with some friends of mine. If you like the smell of sewage over cigarette smoke, then this place is for you.
key_point: The bartender started a shouting match with the reviewer's friends, creating an unpleasant atmosphere.
business_response: We're sorry to hear that our bartender's behavior fell short of our standards. We take incidents like this seriously and will retrain our staff to ensure a respectful environment for all guests. We'd love the opportunity to serve you better in the future.

text: Pomo was an excellent choice for us to celebrate our 5 year anniversary! We went on a Saturday night around 5:30 and there was no wait. Literally 5 minutes after we walked in though we would've been waiting. The service was excellent and the owner even brought us a special anniversary treat on the house! The pizza was delicious too. The bruschetta was very different than any others I've had before. Not bad, just different. We probably wouldn't order it again. The ambiance is also very charming. We had a wonderful time!
key_point: The bruschetta was very different than any others I've had before, and we probably wouldn't order it again
business_response: We're glad you enjoyed your anniversary celebration with us! We appreciate your honest feedback about our bruschetta, and we'll take it into consideration for future menu updates. We'd love to have you back and try some of our other dishes!

text: Good wine selection but the dude named Paul asked me if I need wine glasses or am I going to drink it out of the bottle.  Hu!  I have wine glasses thank you.  Rudeness tells me why he hates working here unless it's a bad joke, probably but still inappropriate.
key_point: Server Paul asked if reviewer needed wine glasses, implying they might drink from the bottle
business_response: We apologize for the misunderstanding with Server Paul. We strive to provide excellent service and appreciate your feedback. We'd love to have you back to experience our wine selection and service in a more welcoming environment.

text: Such a disappointment.  My wife and I are huge Beatles fans.  We have never seen a cirque show before, so we were thrilled to get the chance.  This did not live up to expectation. There were three \"stunts\" that wowed us, but we were expecting a show of that.  It was much more of a dance performance than a circus performance, and one without a coherent message or story at that.
key_point: The show was more of a dance performance than a circus performance with no coherent message or story.
business_response: We're sorry to hear that our Cirque show didn't meet your Beatles-themed expectations. We'll take your feedback into consideration to improve our storytelling and performance balance. We invite you to give us another chance to wow you with our unique blend of circus arts and music.

text: If I could give Mark Taylor no stars I would. All of their leasing clowns get $11.25 hour.\nGreat pay for what they do! I guess they are told to not let U.S. VETS be approved. My credit score was .3 below acceptable for their $99 security deposit. They wanted a $100 more plus rent would go up $40 per month. The 2 things on the credit report were even listed as identity theft. They didn't care. A women named Amanda said tough no refund for the apartment. Mark Taylor is a true SCUMBAG!
key_point: The leasing staff allegedly told the reviewer that they wouldn't approve U.S. VETS due to their credit score, despite the score being only 0.3 below the acceptable threshold.
business_response: We apologize for the disappointing experience with our leasing process. We take all allegations of discriminatory practices seriously and will investigate this matter further. We invite the reviewer to contact us directly so we can discuss their application and work towards a resolution.

text: Pretty good happy hour. Not very authentic sushi. I hate people who think the can say they \"LOVE RA\" but the only thing they get are the California rolls. If you're looking for authentic Japanese sushi- stay away. Fun atmosphere, pretty good service, cheap happy hour, but I've def had better sushi.
key_point: Not very authentic sushi, particularly the California rolls
business_response: We appreciate your feedback on our sushi offerings. We're sorry to hear our California rolls didn't meet your expectations for authenticity. We'd love to have you try our other sushi options and share your thoughts with us.

text: We have been taking our daughter to Dr Gentile since birth. She is now 10 months. We have had a positive experience every time with Dr. Gentile. We only had 1 negative experience  at the Gilbert office with a different doctor at that location. She told me I needed to give my very large baby (9lbs) formula when I was solely breastfeeding. I didn't like that at all especially since I told her that I didn't want to do formula. I decided not to take her advice and see Dr. Gentile instead on our next visit. Dr. Gentile always respects our wishes. \n\nWe are very happy.
key_point: A doctor at the Gilbert office advised breastfeeding a 9lbs baby to supplement with formula, despite the mother's preference to breastfeed exclusively.
business_response: We apologize for the miscommunication at our Gilbert office. We respect our patients' choices and appreciate your trust in Dr. Gentile. We're glad you've had a positive experience with her and look forward to continuing to serve you.

text: Very good, but highly overrated.
key_point: Review finds the business highly overrated
business_response: We appreciate your honest feedback and are sorry to hear our experience didn't meet your expectations. We'll continue to work on providing the best possible service and value. We'd love the chance to show you what we're capable of on a future visit.

text: Great service. Good Pizza. However, the sweat tea needs help.
key_point: the sweat tea needs help
business_response: We're sorry to hear our sweet tea didn't meet your expectations. We'll review our tea preparation process to ensure it's up to our usual standards. We'd love to have you try it again on your next visit.

text:
Cute cute place with good food! Surprising since it's on the first floor of an office building but I'm not judging by the cover;)

We had the cheese fondue as an appetizer which was so so good!!! 
The Mahi entree I had was a little more rare then I'd like but it was a good new experience. I loved the asian cabbage slaw and the sticky rice was divine. There was sauce on the mahi and on the plate but it was not overwhelming at all. They know what they are doing there! I would TOTALLY go back again!!!
key_point: The Mahi entree was a little more rare than the reviewer would have liked
business_response: We appreciate your feedback on the Mahi entree's doneness. We'll make sure to adjust our cooking times to meet our guests' preferences. We're glad you enjoyed the rest of your meal and look forward to serving you again soon.

text: I'm a comics purist. I want to be able to walk into a store and find my comic in pristine condition on the shelf. I want someone to roam the aisles or stack shelves and if I have questions, I want them to enthusiastic and help me. If I spend a lot of money at the counter, i want to be treated like a regular or someone worthy of respect.\n\nInstead, this is my typical experience. I walk in and I see all of  stores employees standing behind the counter and laughing and talking obnoxiously loud with one another.  I don't see anyone looking at comics. The most activity in the store is at the card table. I usually don't find my comic on the shelf because they failed to order enough comics as usual.  I walk up to the counter and wait 3 minutes until the employees finish their conversation with e
key_point: Employees were too busy laughing and talking to restock shelves, and failed to order enough comics, making it hard to find specific issues.
business_response: We apologize for the disappointing experience at Atomic Comics. We're taking steps to ensure our staff is more focused on restocking shelves and ordering comics to meet customer demand. We appreciate your feedback and invite you to visit us again to see the improvements we're making.
